entry.title: Why You Should Start Using the "Customer Return (A2X)" OData V4 API Today
entry.content:
Over the past years, we’ve invested heavily in shaping the next generation of customer return integrations through the Customer Return (A2X) OData V4 API. Compared to the Customer Return (A2X) OData V2 API, it offers greater flexibility, improved capabilities, and ongoing enhancements designed to support more returns scenarios.

Why Start with OData V4?

If you’re beginning a new integration project or exploring customer return APIs for the first time, we highly recommend starting directly with the OData V4 API. It offers:

Continuous Feature Enhancements

V4 is not just another API version—it’s where all new features and future innovations will appear. We are actively updating and expanding V4 to support evolving returns scenarios.

More Flexibility When Processing Customer Returns

V4 provides significantly more functional and technical flexibility than V2, such as:

  • Create returns orders without mandatory subentities, lowering payload complexity
  • Flexibly choose between synchronous or asynchronous modes:
    • Use synchronous mode when efficiency is a priority.
    • Use asynchronous mode when dealing high returns transaction volume.
  • Based on the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model
  • Flexible response handling
  • …..

Already Using OData V2? Why Consider Switching?

If you're currently using the OData V2 API, now is an excellent time to evaluate a move to V4—especially if you plan to support additional integration needs, such as handling configurable materials (AVC), automatic inspection, product valuation suspension, and so on.

While migration may require some upfront adaptation effort, switching from V2 to V4 typically does not mean rebuilding your entire integration. Most existing business logic can still be reused while adapting to the new API structure. Customers can also adopt a phased approach—continuing to run their existing scenarios on V2 while gradually introducing new or enhanced flows on V4. This ensures minimal disruption while unlocking long‑term benefits such as simplified payloads, greater processing flexibility, and access to all future innovations.

V2 vs V4 Feature Comparison

To help you make an informed decision, you can review the complete comparison between V2 and V4 in the help document:

Feature Comparison for Customer Return APIs | SAP Help Portal

Final Recommendation:

If you're:

  • Starting a new integration → Choose OData V4.
  • Expanding current integrations → Consider switching to OData V4.
  • Planning for long-term alignment with SAP strategy → V4 is the standard going forward.

The OData V4 API is already mature, feature-rich, and continuously improving. Now is the time to take advantage of it.

Resources

Help documentAPI hub link
Customer Return (A2X, OData V4)CUSTOMERRETURN

Customer Return (A2X, OData V2)

API_CUSTOMER_RETURN_SRV

entry.title: Managing Upgrades in a Parallel Line in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Best Practices and Tips
entry.content:
Introduction

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, managing software and content upgrades requires extra attention when working with both a main line and a parallel line. Proper planning and execution are important to avoid system conflicts, incomplete configurations, or testing issues. This blog explains key points and simple recommendations to help you handle upgrades smoothly in a parallel line setup.

Understanding Upgrades in Main and Parallel Lines

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, upgrades happen in both the main line and the parallel line. However, there is one important rule:
Features that depend on the latest reference content can only be tested after that content is updated in your test system.

This means you must always ensure that the latest reference content is imported first before testing any new functionality.

Avoid Risky Timing

One common mistake is starting activities like Rebase or Merge on Friday afternoons.

Why avoid this?
SAP often schedules upgrades and updates over the weekend. If your activities overlap with these updates, it can cause:

  • System inconsistencies
  • Interrupted upgrades
  • Failed processes

Recommendation: Plan rebases and merges earlier in the week.

Manage Staging Entries Carefully

Before any upgrade or update:

  • Check if there are Customer staging entries with status “Activation in Progress”
  • Do not proceed until these are finalized

You should manually update their status to:

  • Staging Completed, or
  • Staging Cancelled

What happens if you don’t?

SAP will automatically mark them as Staging Completed and continue the upgrade. However:

  • The entries may not actually be finished
  • They may still appear as Activation in Progress later
  • This can create confusion during future rebases

Best Practice: Always finalize staging entries manually.

Content Upgrade Behavior

Content upgrades are handled separately for:

  • Main line
  • Parallel line

This means each line can be upgraded at different times.

However, there is a difference in flexibility:

  • Main line: You can change transport automation settings
  • Parallel line: Uses default settings only (no changes allowed)

Recommendation for Content Upgrades

Even though SAP allows upgrades at different times, it is strongly recommended to:

  • Upgrade both main and parallel lines at the same time

Why?

Upgrading at different times can lead to:

  • Configuration mismatches
  • Testing inconsistencies
  • Unexpected system behavior

Best Practice: Keep both lines aligned by upgrading them simultaneously.

Conclusion

Managing upgrades in a parallel line setup requires attention to timing, staging status, and consistency between systems. By following simple practices—like avoiding Friday changes, completing staging entries, and synchronizing upgrades—you can ensure a smooth and error-free upgrade process in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition systems.

Keeping both your main and parallel lines aligned will help maintain system stability and improve overall project efficiency.

entry.title: Attachment and note Support in App Manage Automatic Payments
entry.content:
Enhancing Executive Approval Experience:

Why Attachment and Note Support Matters in Manage Automatic Payments

In today’s ERP cloud landscape, automation alone is not enough. What truly defines enterprise readiness is whether an end-to-end business process works seamlessly across all user roles, including the most senior decision makers.

This is exactly why the new SAP Influence idea
👉 “Attachment and Note Support in Manage Automatic Payments”
(described in https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/370881/?section=sectionDetails)
is so critical.

The Bigger Picture: Automatic Payments Are an End-to-End Process

The Manage Automatic Payments app plays a central role in financial operations. From proposal generation to payment execution, it is designed to support highly automated scenarios.

However, in real customer environments, the process does not end with payment creation.

Automatic payments typically trigger multi-level approval workflows, involving:

  • Finance managers
  • CFOs
  • CEOs
  • In some cases, company owners or board-level executives

These approvals are not formalities. They are compliance-critical, audit-relevant, and decision-heavy steps.

The Gap Today: Missing Context During Approval

For executive approvers, context is everything.

When approving high-value or sensitive payment runs, they often need:

  • Supporting attachments (e.g. contracts, invoices, audit documents)
  • Clear notes or explanations describing:
    • Why the payment is triggered
    • What exceptions exist
    • What risks or urgencies apply

Without attachment and note support directly in Manage Automatic Payments, approvers face real challenges:

  • They must leave the app and search for context elsewhere
  • They rely on offline communication (emails, meetings, screenshots)
  • Decision-making becomes slower, fragmented, and less transparent

This breaks the promise of end-to-end automation and weakens trust in the digital approval process.

Why This Matters Even More for Executive Users

CFOs, CEOs, and business owners are often:

  • Infrequent ERP users
  • Extremely sensitive to usability and clarity
  • Focused on risk, compliance, and accountability

At the same time, they are:

  • Key decision makers for purchasing ERP cloud solutions
  • Strong influencers of customer perception and platform adoption

If their approval experience feels incomplete or inconvenient, it directly impacts:

  • Confidence in SAP’s enterprise capabilities
  • Acceptance of further automation
  • Willingness to expand ERP cloud usage

In other words, their experience matters disproportionally.

The Idea: Attachment and Note Support in Manage Automatic Payments

The proposed idea introduces:

  • ✅ Attachment support to provide formal documentation
  • ✅ Note support to add business context and explanations

This enhancement would:

  • Strengthen auditability and compliance
  • Improve transparency and decision quality
  • Enable smoother, faster executive approvals
  • Preserve the integrity of the end-to-end automatic payment flow

Most importantly, it aligns perfectly with how customers actually operate their financial approval processes.

High Impact, High Visibility

This is not a niche requirement.

It is a cross-cutting improvement that:

  • Touches finance operations
  • Influences approval workflows
  • Impacts C-level experience
  • Shapes customer perception of SAP ERP Cloud

For these reasons, Attachment and Note Support deserves high priority in the evolution of Manage Automatic Payments.

Call to Action

If you believe that:

  • End-to-end processes matter
  • Executive users deserve first-class UX
  • Automatic payments should truly be enterprise-ready

👉 Please Vote to Idea #370881 on SAP Influence and help move this enhancement forward.

Together, we can ensure that Manage Automatic Payments delivers not only automation, but also confidence, clarity, and trust—from operational users all the way up to the CXO.

entry.title: Error KI154 in Manage My Timesheet: How to Resolve Activity Type and Cost Center Issues
entry.content:
In this blog post, I would like to share an issue we encountered in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud that prevented users from recording their working time using the Manage My Timesheet app. This article may help others facing similar problems related to error KI154.

 

Issue:

Some users were unable to record their time in the Manage My Timesheet app. After creating a timesheet entry, the following error message was displayed: 

  • Enter activity type only in conjunction with cost center (KI154) (in English)
  • Geben Sie die Leistungsart nur in Verbindung mit einer Kostenstelle an (KI154)  (in German)

 

 

 

Root Cause:

Due to the setup of the interface between SAP S/4HANA and the HR system, each master data change created a new record while removing the cost center and manager from the previous one. When users attempted to record time for an older time period, that record no longer contained a cost center, which triggered error KI154.

 

Solution:

To resolve the issue, perform the following steps: 
1. Open the Manage Workforce app. 
2. Check the employee’s Work Agreement. 
3. Ensure that a Cost Center is maintained for the relevant time period. 
4. If required, temporarily reverse the changes that removed the cost center. 
5. After the timesheet entries have been recorded, the changes can be applied again.

 

Summary:

Error KI154 occurs when a user records time for a period in which no cost center is assigned in the employee master data. Ensuring that the cost center is correctly maintained for the relevant time slot will resolve the issue. 

References:

  • 2647776 - Manage My Timesheet - Error Enter activity type only in conjunction with cost center. - SAP for Me
  • Solved: In Manage My Timesheet unable to Create Task - SAP Community

entry.title: Custom Business Configurations (F4579): Collaborative draft
entry.content:
Introduction

The Custom Business Configurations (CUBCO) app serves as an entry point to the Business Configuration Maintenance Object (SMBC) provided by custom applications or partners.

When you use the ADT wizard to generate a Business Configuration Maintenance Object, the singleton pattern is used when generating the draft enabled RAP BO. One attribute of this pattern is that all entities are locked, so it is not possible for multiple users to work with the same configuration at the same time.

This blog describes how to avoid this restriction by using collaborative drafts.

This blog is relevant for

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 
  • SAP BTP ABAP environment 
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

Further reading:

  • Related blog posts
  • Learn how you can use ABAP technology to develop innovative applications and business solutions across SAP’s portfolio on SAP Learning Site.

Example scenario

In the configuration table ZDEMO_CC, the company codes are stored.

@EndUserText.label : 'Company Code'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #C
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #ALLOWED
define table zdemo_cc {
  key client            : abap.clnt not null;
  key company_code      : abap.numc(4) not null;
  country               : land1;
  last_changed_at       : abp_lastchange_tstmpl;
  local_last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;
}

Enable collaborative draft

In the generated behavior definition, add "collaborative" to the draft annotation:

managed with additional save implementation in class ZBP_I_COMPANYCODE_S unique;
strict;
with collaborative draft;

Add the draft query annotation and the share action to the singleton entity definition:

define behavior for ZI_CompanyCode_S alias CompanyCodeAll
draft table ZDEMO_CC_D_S query ZR_DEMO_CC_CD
[...]
{
  draft action Share;
  [...]
}

 Right-click the singleton draft table and choose New Data Definition to create the CDS view for the draft query:

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Draft Query View'
define view entity ZR_DEMO_CC_CD
  as select from zdemo_cc_d_s
{
  key singletonid                   as Singletonid,
      lastchangedatmax              as Lastchangedatmax,
      transportrequestid            as Transportrequestid,
      draftentitycreationdatetime   as Draftentitycreationdatetime,
      draftentitylastchangedatetime as Draftentitylastchangedatetime,
      draftadministrativedatauuid   as Draftadministrativedatauuid,
      draftentityoperationcode      as Draftentityoperationcode,
      hasactiveentity               as Hasactiveentity,
      draftfieldchanges             as Draftfieldchanges
}

You need to enhance the authorization of the involved users. In SAP BTP ABAP environment and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition you assign business catalog SAP_CORE_BC_RAP_DRAFT_PC to the user, in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition authorization object S_DRAFT.

User TB is creating a new company code 400 while at the same time user PW is editing company code 100. All changes are recorded on the same transport request.

Since the collaborative draft is enabled for the singleton entity, the save and discard draft action are applied to all changes. In practice, this means that discarding the draft or saving must be coordinated between the users involved.
For completely independent editing of individual objects, you must develop a RAP BO without a singleton pattern.
